item.title: Devil May Cry Anime Helps DMC5 Achieve Record Sales Ahead Of Switch 2 Launch
item.content:encoded:
SSS - Stylish!

Capcom has recently confirmed the launch of Devil May Cry 5: Devil Hunter Edition for the Switch 2, bringing one of the finest entries in the action series to Nintendo's console more than seven years after its initial launch.

You'd think that being on the market for that long would result in gradually diminishing sales, but according to the latest figures from Capcom (thanks, VGC), Devil May Cry 5 has enjoyed record sales during FY2026, shifting a total of 2.7 million copies.

When we say "record sales", we really mean it. The 2.7 million figure is actually higher than DMC5's launch year performance, during which it managed to sell 2.1 million. Annual sales then declined until FY2023 in which it matched the launch figure with another 2.1 million. Sales then began to decline again until FY2026 when it shifted record units.

Capcom tends to discount its games pretty heavily after a certain amount of time, and a quick glance at Deku Deals shows that Devil May Cry 5: Special Edition for the PS5 often drops as low as £8.74 on the PlayStation Store, while retail copies have previously dropped as low as £12.99.

This, along with the release of the Devil May Cry anime on Netflix – which recently added Season Two in its entirety – has likely contributed greatly to overall sales in recent months. Now that the game is heading for the Switch 2, we reckon Capcom can probably enjoy even more sales in the months and years ahead.

Resident Evil 4 also came close to matching its launch year sales in FY2026 by shifting another 3.6 million units, while Street Fighter 6 also saw year-on-year improvements by shifting a total of 2 million copies during FY2026.

Devil May Cry 5: Devil Hunter Edition will launch for the Switch 2 on 23rd June 2026.

Have you played DMC5 before, or are you waiting to experience it on the Switch 2? Let us know with a comment.

[source capcom.co.jp, via videogameschronicle.com]

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/devil-may-cry-anime-helps-dmc5-achieve-record-sales-ahead-of-switch-2-launch

item.title: The First KPop Demon Hunters Lego Set Has Finally Been Revealed And It Looks Great
item.content:encoded:
After a tease earlier this year, Lego has finally revealed the first of its KPop Demon Hunters collaboration sets starring none other than the Derpy the demon tiger and Sussie the hat-wearing demon bird.

The 825-piece set will launch on August 1 and run you $70. Derpy himself has two “expressions,” one being his standard wide-eyed stare while the other has his tongue out, presenting the invitation cards that Jinu and Rumi send to one another to set up secret meetings throughout the film. Sussie, meanwhile, is a bit more straightforward, and can be perched on top of Derpy’s head. The set also includes a flower pot, which anyone who’s seen KPop Demon Hunters knows is the tiger’s worst enemy, as he accidentally knocks them over during the movie and has no thumbs to help him set them back up.

Lego says that the set is meant for both display and play, meaning kids can actually run around with the big cat for play time, and I still think that is a crazy thing to assert for something that is $70 and hundreds of pieces. That thing is going up on a shelf so high, small children will have to crane their necks to even see it, but y’all live your lives, I guess. 

More KPop Demon Hunters Lego sets are planned for next year, though we don’t know what they’ll entail just yet. It’s very likely we’ll see sets based on the main Huntr/x trio, and maybe the evil boy band the Saja Boys as well. Hopefully there’ll be some minifigs in the future, because I’d buy a Rumi one to put on my shelf without a second thought.

KPop Demon Hunters is approaching its first anniversary next month, with Netflix planning to bring the movie back to theaters once more. A sequel is officially in the works, with some kind of live tour also planned in the near future.



https://kotaku.com/kpop-demon-hunters-lego-derpy-sussie-order-release-date-2000707071

item.title: Banned horror title Horses begins recouping development costs after selling 30,000 copies
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 16, 2026

2 Min Read

Santa Ragione's surreal horror title Horses, which was banned on Steam and the Epic Games Store, has begun recouping its development costs after selling 30,000 copies worldwide and generating $100,000 in revenue.

In an email sent to Game Developer, Santa Ragione co-founder and studio director Pietro Righi Riva explained the Italian company doesn't have enough cash to kickstart production on another project but has managed to successfully pay back its private investors.

"For anyone curious about where that went: of the $100k, $30k went to the game's director, Andrea Lucco Borlera, who spent years working on it in his own time, and $62k went to the game's private funders, covering their $50k investment and providing a return," said Riva. "That leaves $8k for Santa Ragione. It isn't enough to begin anything new, but it's a start toward recouping our $50k in development costs."

Santa Ragione previously said it was facing closure after Horses was banned from Steam over content platform owner Valve described as concerning. After Epic also decided to block Horses from launching on the Epic Game Store, the title eventually made its debut on Itch.io, GOG, Humble Store. 

Related:UK social media ban will restrict 'high-risk' features on game platforms

Horses went on to win multiple awards including the 2026 IGF Nuovo Award and Best Experience at the Italian Video Game Awards.

Riva said he is "eternally grateful" to everybody who supported the studio over the years and hopes the difficulties surrounding the distribution of Horses sparked important conversations around censorship.

"Horses uses grotesque, subversive imagery to confront power, faith, and violence. We reject subjective obscenity standards and believe this kind of moralizing censorship evokes a darker past in which vague notions of 'decency' were used to silence artists. Games are an artistic medium and lawful works for adults should remain accessible," explained the studio," said Santa Ragione in a statement when Horses was banned on Steam. 

"We respect players enough to present the game as intended and to let adults choose what to play; lawful works should not be made unreachable by a monopolistic storefront’s opaque decisions."

The final update for Horses was rolled out this week and brings Japanese, Korean, Spanish, German, French, and Russian language support. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/horses-begins-recouping-development-costs-after-selling-30-000-copies

item.title: Ubisoft Ends Assassin’s Creed Shadows Support With Black Tides, the True Finale Hunting Naoe and Yasuke at Last
item.content:encoded:
Today, Ubisoft is releasing the final content drop update for Assassin's Creed Shadows. Patch 1.1.11, which varies in size from the 9GB of the Mac version to the 26.1GB of the Xbox version, introduces new story content, a major endgame feature, live crossover events, and a wide range of bug fixes.

First, there's a new quest called Black Tides, which serves as the true concluding chapter for protagonists Naoe and Yasuke. Two elite Templars known as the Black Cross arrive in Japan to hunt the protagonists for the chaos they've caused, though both agents may be pursuing their own hidden agendas. To unlock the quest, players must have completed the main stories of both protagonists, as well as the two previous post-launch story drops (A Critical Encounter, which introduced rogue Rufino, and A Puzzlement, which added Kane and Kinu). Notably, having finished the Claws of Awaji expansion is not required. For players who skipped the post-launch content entirely, this effectively means there is now a three-chapter story arc waiting before this final quest, making it the de facto "real" ending of the game.

Then there's Domains, the headline new feature for hardcore players, accessible at level 30. Created in-world by MOD, an Assassin hacker, as a set of combat and survival trials for Dark Animus users, it consists of 5 gameplay simulation arenas across 10 escalating difficulty levels, with the challenge designed to force players to rethink their builds: a strategy that works in one Domain may fail entirely in the next. The feature comes with its own dedicated merchant, in-game currency, and progression tree where players can purchase Animus exploits, new endgame gear sets, powerful engravings, and fan-favorite legacy weapons. Crucially, none of these rewards will ever appear in the store or the exchange, as they are exclusively earned through Domains and intended as pure bragging-rights items for the most dedicated players.

A third Animus Rift called Horizon is also now available, continuing the Eagle/Guide storyline. Players must have completed both previous Animus Rifts to access it.

Several Assassin's Creed Shadows combat adjustments are included in Patch 1.1.11, too:

  • Damage boost for Shurikens fired from the Bank Shot and Triple Threat mastery upgrades
  • Armor-destroying abilities are now half as effective against bosses
  • Enemies now take fall damage from shorter heights, increasing overall fall damage
  • Damage from enemy Poison Kunai and Explosive Grenades is reduced
  • Up to 6 Corrupted Castles are now available each season

A batch of adrenaline-related perks have been rebalanced to percentage-based values rather than fixed ones, including Adrenaline on Headshot, Adrenaline on Critical Hit, and several others. Key item buffs include:

  • Whisper of Gold Long Katana perk now grants 100% ability damage (up from 50%)
  • Ethereal Cloth Headgear perk now triggers at below 15% health (up from 10%)
  • Pink Petal Kosode and Sakura Bloom Armor now convert adrenaline to 20% health on death (up from 10%)
  • Teppo and Bow weapons can now be engraved with armor damage and armor-piercing perks

Finally, the Animus HUB is now accessed exclusively through the Shadows Memory page in the system menu, harmonizing in-menu and in-gameplay navigation. The Forge engraving list has been reordered with a new Critical Category added, and world map pins and markers now correctly persist through travel between Japan and Awaji. On that note, the patch also adds two new crossover Projects in the Animus HUB, Riptides and Undertow, which unlock themed outfits, weapons, trinkets, and other rewards. Players activate them by completing Anomalies and progressing through the reward tracks, just like previous HUB projects. These rewards span both Assassin’s Creed Shadows and the upcoming Black Flag Resynced, so progress can be made by playing Anomalies in either game. One important limitation is that the crossover Projects will not be available on Nintendo Switch 2, since the game isn't coming to the console.

Did you enjoy your time with Assassin's Creed Shadows? Let us know by leaving a comment below.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/assassins-creed-shadows-black-tides-finale/

item.title: Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival launches October 8
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Survival horror action game dated.

Survival horror action game Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam on October 8, Saber Interactive and Boss Team Games announced.

The game will be available in the following editions:

  • Standard Edition ($39.99) – physical or digital
  • Deluxe Edition ($49.99)
    • Full details on the Deluxe Edition’s contents will be revealed closer to launch.

Pre-orders for any edition of the game will unlock the exclusive in-game Labyrinth Pistol Skin and Martyr Sawblade Skin at release.

Here is an overview of the game, via Saber Interactive:

About

Embark on a battle for survival against Hell’s armies with a terrifying new story in the Hellraiser universe in Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival! Descend into the darkest depths of the underworld to rescue your love from the depraved clutches of the Cenobites. Face your deepest fears against deviant wretches, cultists and creatures with the infernal powers of the Genesis Configuration puzzle box and an arsenal of earthly weapons.

Key Features

  • A New Nightmare in the Hellraiser Universe – Discover a chilling new story in the iconic horror franchise as you unwittingly kickstart Pinhead’s (voiced by Doug Bradley) ritual to unleash the ultimate torment on the world. We assure you: the pain will be exquisite.
  • Wield the Might of Hell – Harness the unique powers of the Genesis Configuration, including pyrokinesis, telekinesis, and the iconic Hell Chains, to inflict bone-breaking, flesh-searing pain (or pleasure?) upon your enemies. The Genesis Configuration is also instrumental in solving puzzles and transforming parts of the winding Labyrinth.
  • Battle for Survival Against Hell’s Armies – Take on Hell’s wretches, deviants, cultists, and its most infernal priests in an unholy war for your immortal soul, all while trying to thwart the sinister plans of the Cenobites.

Watch the trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/clive-barkers-hellraiser-revival-launches-october-8

item.title: Sony has patented a controller with buttons that harden when you play with it
item.content:encoded:
Sony Interactive Entertainment has published an intriguing patent for a new type of controller whose buttons can alter their resistance and tactile feel - becoming harder or softer - to adapt to what's being shown on screen. Sony initially filed the patent application with the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO), the United Nations' patents agency, in November 2024. It was officially published in May this year, as uncovered by Cheat Happens.

According to the patent, the controller would adapt - either based on gameplay or personal preferences - by changing the hardness and resistance of the underlying mechanisms that respond to each button press. This is achieved using a magneto-viscoelastic elastomer, a material capable of hardening and softening based on inputs from attached magnets.

Said magnets can be controlled by the game, creating a magnetic field that varies in strength based on the action on screen. Sony's designs also indicate potentially utilising fluid-filled membranes to achieve a similar effect.

Elsewhere in the patent, there's a look at a different design that allows the player's finger to "sink" into the button itself, and for the button to harden around it. This could perhaps be used to simulate being grabbed by an enemy, or getting stuck in mud, though the usage isn't entirely clear from the language.

Sony's proposed designs call back to the patents that preceded the unveiling of the PS5's DualSense controller. The DualSense is unique in multiple ways, chiefly in its use of haptic motors and tiny speakers to produce a level of tactile feedback no other controller has. The adaptive triggers were similarly innovative, enabling developers to, for instance, tune how hard the player needs to squeeze based on the weapon being used in-game.

Though Sony's newly unearthed controller design, with its adjustable hardness, may seem a little difficult to image now, Sony has already confirmed it's working on its next-generation PlayStation. So if the patent is granted, it could be a core feature of an updated DualSense controller.

Although Sony hasn't decided on a price or launch date for the PS6 yet, current reports suggest a 2028 or 2029 release for the console, which is far enough away that the intriguing tech could make it into a production unit.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/sony-controller-soft-hard-buttons-patent

item.title: PUBG Beta Testing New AI-Focused ‘Ally Duo’ Game Mode, Dates Revealed
item.content:encoded:
One of the OG battle royale shooters is about to broaden its horizons even further, as PlayerUnknown’s Battlegrounds, AKA PUBG, is allowing players to test out the new Ally Duo mode, which sees you battle in various scenarios and situations.

Can you believe that PUBG is nearly 10 years old? The battle royale phenomenon is still kicking, and hundreds of thousands of players still hop on daily. Krafton and PUBG Studios are keeping the shooter alive and well, and it’s time to welcome Ally Duo to the fold.

If you haven’t already, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android:

  • Android Link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.insidergaming.app
  • iOS Link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/insider-gaming/id6753984648

PUBG Ally Duo Beta Test Runs From June 17 to July 1

This is being built up as an evolution of PUBG. The publisher has said, ‘PUBG Ally uses speech-to-text (STT) and text-to-speech (TTS) technologies to interact with players in real time,’ and it’s believed this can create a new experience never before seen or played.

The beta takes place from available from 17 June to 1 July, so you’ve got plenty of time to see if this breakthrough is for you.

  • More Insider Gaming News: Hellraiser: Revival Release Date Revealed in New Trailer

A press release from Krafton explained to us:

‘Ally Duo is a new Arcade mode in which players form a two-player team with the AI character, Ella, and play on the Sanhok map. Players can communicate with Ella via voice interaction to collaborate across a range of in-game situations, including movement, item collection, and strategy. This mode allows players to team up with an AI companion, creating a uniquely immersive, cooperative gameplay experience.

Ella is the companion character built on PUBG Ally, KRAFTON’s AI technology. PUBG Ally is a new type of CPC (Co-Playable Character) designed to play alongside human players. Powered by an on-device small language model built on NVIDIA ACE, it is designed to understand players’ voice commands and in-game situations to respond proactively.’

Could this pave the way for a new future of AI-influenced game modes that conventional playlist options simply can’t offer? People are sure to have opinions about this.

Learn even more about the innovative game mode below.

Let us know in the Insider Gaming Discord if you’re considering trying PUBG’s latest craze!

Check out TBH: Task Bar Hero challenging Counter-Strike 2’s dominance on Steam for more news. For more Insider Gaming content delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: More than a thousand games in Steam Next Fest feature a generative AI disclosure of some kind
item.content:encoded:
Steam Next Fest is back on the PC's largest gaming store, offering a vast selection of demos for upcoming games. And many of these, it turns out, have generative AI disclosures present.

SteamDB, a stat-tracking database, allows you to filter the full list of Steam Next Fest games using various tags. With this, we can see there are 8700 games participating in the Steam Next Fest event, and 1704 of those have the generative AI tag attached, as of the time of writing. That's roughly 20 percent of the games taking part.

Steam as a storefront has been steadfast in requiring games to declare their use of generative AI during development, only altering it slightly to allow for AI-enabled "efficiency gains" to go undeclared. Even then, it stressed that the technology's use must be declared to players.

This mandatory declaration for AI has forced some developers into hot water. Only recently, Sega had to clarify how generative AI was used in the new Crazy Taxi game. First, it was said that generative AI was used to create background assets. Then, at Summer Game Fest, this changed to only being used as a reference.

The technology remains controversial among gaming enthusiasts. It is at the heart of the current computer hardware price spike, as components used for consoles and gaming computers are in shorter supply. Still, that has not stopped big releases from using it, such as Crimson Desert, and from companies such as Arc Raiders developer Embark supporting it.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/steam-next-fest-generative-ai-disclosure-thousands

item.title: Review: Rise Of The Tomb Raider: 20 Year Celebration (Switch 2) - The Crown Jewel Of The Survivor Trilogy
item.content:encoded:
In 2025, Aspyr surprised players with a shadow drop of the 2013 Tomb Raider reboot on Switch 1 & 2, with the latter port especially showcasing the power of Nintendo’s new handheld. This sudden release naturally spurred discussion over whether there would be ports of the latter two games in the Survivor Trilogy.

Though the jury’s still out on whether we’ll ever see a port of Shadow of the Tomb Raider, it seems a lot more likely now that Aspyr shadow-dropped Rise of the Tomb Raider: 20 Year Celebration during the June Nintendo Direct.

While the more advanced graphics of the sequel contribute to a slight hit to performance, this is an overall solid port of arguably the best entry of the Survivor Trilogy. Lara’s sophomore outing feels like a refinement of the strengths in its predecessor, combining big set-piece action sequences with more pensive puzzle-solving in the eponymous tombs. Tie it all together with Metroidvania-esque exploration, gorgeous graphics, and all the original DLC bundled in, and you’ve got a great package that offers a lot of bang for your buck.

Rise of the Tomb Raider is set about a year after Tomb Raider (2013), following Lara’s adventures in Siberia pursuing a mysterious MacGuffin called the Divine Source, which supposedly holds the key to eternal life. But Lara isn’t alone in this pursuit: an ancient shadowy organisation called Trinity wants the Divine Source for its own purposes and will stop at nothing to find it first.

Luckily for Lara, they don’t have the same expertise in ancient history as she does, and as she races against them to uncover its location, her search is aided as she learns more about the life of an ancient messiah figure named The Prophet, whose history is inextricably linked with the Divine Source.

It’s not exactly the most memorable narrative, yet the plot does a great job of giving you the ‘summer blockbuster’ feel as our intrepid heroine overcomes impossible odds and show-stopping set-piece moments. Lara isn’t the most charismatic character, but she goes through some satisfying character development throughout her journey to becoming the titular adventurer we all know and love.

Meanwhile, a cruel Trinity leader named Constantine plays the role of the main villain, stealing many of the scenes he shows up in with his cold demeanour and unrelenting commitment to his goal. He’s easily the most punchable person in the game and is a highlight of the story; a rather simple and irredeemable villain such as him feels like the perfect foil for Lara.

Falling in line with series expectations, the main gameplay loop here ultimately focuses on heavy exploration with some combat encounters peppered in to mix up the pace and put some pressure on the player. It’s not exactly an open-world game, but the game world features a couple of expansive hubs that you frequently return to after finishing side content, and these hubs feature plenty of secrets and collectibles to snap up between missions.

As you complete tombs, gather collectibles, and kill enemies by the dozens, you also gather experience points and resources you can then use to upgrade Lara and her gear back at a camp.

The highlight of the experience here is easily the optional challenge tombs, which are both more frequent and more interesting than those found in this game’s predecessor. Each of these tombs is designed somewhat like a miniature dungeon from the older Zelda games, built around a central gimmick or puzzle element that you need to decipher to get to a treasure at the end. One of my favourites — the Ancient Cistern — is built around a high central tower surrounded by water, and Lara needs to puzzle out how to gradually raise the water level so she can reach the treasure at the top.

Not only is each tomb memorable and distinct, but they all largely manage to hit that fine balancing point between challenge and simplicity. When you finally figure out what’s needed to solve the puzzle, there’s a wonderful sense of satisfaction that comes from finally reaching the end, and this is reinforced by you also being given some sort of new skill or ability.

Even when not exploring a tomb, exploration feels rewarding given that each hub exchanges sheer size for content density. There are hidden relics, treasure maps, caches, and huntable animals seemingly every few feet, routinely rewarding the player for scouring every corner.

There are also some side quests from NPCs that usually prompt you to return to previously completed areas, giving you new challenges to overcome while granting an opportunity to mop up any collectibles you may have missed the first time around. This is the sort of well-paced game that does a great job of both giving you lots of rewarding things to explore and uncover while also not hosing you down with meaningless content bloat and empty activities.

Combat, on the other hand, doesn’t fare nearly as well. While stealth sections are satisfyingly tense and give you plenty of options for distracting and taking down foes, enemy encounters tend to fall apart when the bullets start flying. Though Lara has a solidly versatile and growing arsenal of weapons to respond with, the shooting controls feel too stiff and are rather unsatisfying to navigate using the Joy-Con.

This is something that would hopefully be solved with the newly added gyro controls, but they maddeningly only work when examining relics in Lara’s inventory - gyro controls seemingly don’t apply at all to shooting sections.

You can somewhat get around the control issue by enabling mouse mode, but this itself proves to be awkward due to how the Joy-Con simply doesn’t feel great in the hand as a mouse and still requires you to utilise the face buttons even when held on its side.

The good news is that combat encounters are only maybe 30% of the total experience—they’re a core part of the gameplay loop, but you’ll spend the vast majority of your time crawling around tombs and digging up relics. And while the shooting controls are disappointing, fights usually don’t last long enough to overstay their welcome and really highlight the deficiencies.

This being the ‘complete edition’ of a decade-old game, there’s a decent amount of additional content from later DLC additions that’s been seamlessly integrated into the core game. The highlights are some story-focused quest chains that task you with exploring the famed Croft Manor and with tracking down the Baba Yaga witch in a new tomb, both of which feel like a natural extension of the best parts of the original campaign.

Additionally, there are some more arcade-like additions such as a roguelite mode and a zombie survival mode, both of which help to remix existing content and give you a bit more to chew on once you’ve hit 100% completion.

Regarding the graphical presentation, Rise of the Tomb Raider showcases the strengths of the Switch 2 hardware through the stunning visuals on display here. The grimy, crumbling environments of the tombs are fully realised with impressive detail, as features like cobwebs hanging off skeletons and the soft light emanating from Lara’s glow stick help to give the environments meaningful texture and contribute lots to the immersion. Character models are also well-detailed and animated, with the much-touted TressFX hair making an appearance here for realistic motion as Lara’s ponytail sways with the action.

All this runs at a smooth 1080p and 30fps in both docked and handheld, with no significant drops to be found even in the busier sections. Granted, it’s a little disappointing Aspyr couldn’t get this one to 60fps (even in docked mode) to match its predecessor, but this game was a notable visual step-up when it first launched back in the day and a stable 30fps still feels adequate for the gameplay. As is often the case for these kinds of ports, the absolute best performance and presentation is traded for the ability to play the game portably with no content cuts.

Conclusion

Rise of the Tomb Raider is a great addition to the Switch 2 library, standing as a strong example of the vision of the platform offering full-fat home console gaming on the go. Satisfying exploration, incredible visuals, and loads of content all running at a smooth 30fps combine to make this a must-have buy for your personal library, even if a forgettable story and some occasionally wonky controls hold this one back from perfection.

I’d suggest you pick this one up at the next available opportunity – it’ll keep you busy for a while and is especially the kind of game that really shines in handheld.



https://www.nintendolife.com/reviews/nintendo-switch-2/rise-of-the-tomb-raider-20-year-celebration
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival Is A Wild Trip Of Brutal Combat And Raunchy Horror
item.content:encoded:
Horror as a genre has become larger and larger in the Video Game space, from indie titles to large franchises releasing explosive sequels. At Summer Game Fest this year, we had the chance to sit down and play through a portion of Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival.

In Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival you play as Aiden, a man who, after opening the puzzle box known as the Genesis Configuration while taking part in some very gore-oriented kink-play, had to watch as his girlfriend was taken/killed by the Cenobites that were summoned.

As the name implies, Clive Barker, writer of the Hellraiser books, is a core member of the creative team for Revival. In addition to that, Doug Bradley, the actor who originally brought the Hell Priest Cenobite to life, is reprising his role, voicing the character.

My gameplay demo jumped around to a few different parts of the story to make sure that I was able to get a good taste of everything that the final game will have to offer. For the first segment of the game, which I was told takes place a few hours into the game, Aiden found his way into a BDSM club and needed to progress through it for a reason that was unknown to me.

Cultists In A Sex Club

The set decoration in this location was phenomenal. An entrance hall with vaulted ceilings and flaming braziers decked out in shining golds and velvety reds screamed ‘sex club’. Stepping into the side rooms, you were greeted with changing rooms decked out in just about anything leather or with straps that you could put on, around, or in someone.

Moving through this space, it was clear that Aiden was an intruder, as the workers at this bar were quick to escalate and attempt to kill you. At this point, Aiden’s arsenal included a variety of weapons: a machete, a pistol, and a shotgun (there was also an SMG that could be picked up in a back room), but also the power of the Cube.

Dropped metal objects could be summoned with the cube and fired off at will. You could also draw the blazing fire towards you, drawing it into the cube to then shoot at an enemy. These environmental uses of the puzzle box came up quite frequently in a small space, but once you had summoned an item to yourself, it would only stay for a short amount of time before dropping or flaring out. I imagine there are going to be players who really get into a flow state, combining regular ranged attacks, melee attacks, and the environment to decimate some opponents.

Once things really kicked off, and a variety of partially armored enemies jumped out of Iron Maidens, the combat really kicked it up a notch, as did the difficulty. Aiden is not a character who is able to handle much. It wasn’t long at all before I was staring at a game-over screen.

Starting again, and with the knowledge that you really need to keep your distance, I was able to defeat each of the enemies in the room. Many were wearing metal masks, assumedly because they got off on it, and so during my first attempt at the room, I tried to aim at their exposed flesh. On the repeat attempt, I tried to shoot the metal directly and found that I could shoot it off in a very satisfying manner.

I think there was still some work to do on the ragdoll animations of the deceased enemies falling. With each one I killed, their limbs would all extend at once, causing them to almost spring like a frog for a moment before collapsing to the ground. After the tense combat, it was a much-appreciated moment of levity, though.

While I did have a melee option, I didn’t end up using it, instead favoring the guns in my arsenal. I felt the melee was a bit too difficult to risk getting in close; a shotgun blast from an enemy would very quickly put me on my ass, and I didn’t want to be a sitting duck for another of these crazed cultists to come up and grab me.

While I was only just getting used to the controls, there were some nice quality of life mechanics to keep your head on a swivel. There is a jump back button that lets you very quickly try to make some distance between you and a would-be attacker, on top of that, there was a quick pivot key too, letting you immediately rotate 180 degrees.

After making my way through the sex club, I entered into a puzzle-oriented part of the demo.

Puzzles of geometry and P.T.-esc hellscapes

I was immediately dropped into a misty stone corridor, the signature Hellraised eerie blue light shining through the gaps towards me. With the only way to move ahead of me, I continued turning left and right in what felt like a maze, even though it was a linear experience.

After a few turns, the hallway expanded out into a large chamber where I had to get from my side of the room to the other across a series of narrow stone platforms. This room was a barrel broken up into three segments that I was on the inside of. I didn’t just have to make my way across, but I also had to rotate the three sections to ensure that the path I was hoping to take would line up to get me to freedom. Taking the wrong path would lead me to a dead end, and that’s before we even talk about the spinning blades coming out of the walls at odd intervals.

The final portion of the demo was still puzzle-oriented, but leaned heavily into the psychological horror of titles like P.T or Layers of Fear. As Aiden, I was stuck in a loop of a hallway, in what I can only assume is Aiden’s house. I had the ability to go down two hallways, up the stairs, and through a door before I’d return to the start of the first hallway.

You needed to pick up on key differences, follow the prompts in the vocal cues, and interact with objects that prompted Aiden to talk about the relationship with his girlfriend, and reveal the kinds of fantasies he had about her.

There is a LOT of sex, nudity, and hardcore kink play here, so this game will definitely not be for the squeamish.

While the experience I had was slightly jarring between tough, fast-paced combat, eerie logic puzzles, and horror-inspired puzzling it was a good encapsulation of everything that this game will have to offer when it releases.

Andrew has written Video Game and Entertainment news, reviews, and guides for 10+ years. As Senior Content Manager, he assists in creating and editing… More about Andrew



https://techraptor.net/gaming/previews/clive-barkers-hellraiser-revival-is-wild-trip-of-brutal-combat-and-raunchy-horror

item.title: EA, the big brains behind The Sims 2 H&M Fashion Stuff, announce they're "transforming how brands connect with audiences" – with in-game billboards
item.content:encoded:
Just think of the synergy

In the time-old tradition of marketing, EA have announced something ancient as though it's just been thought up by the boffins in the advertising lab. Their big new moneymaking wheeze to squeeze every last penny out of their games: billboards.

In a press release that stretches into essay-length territory, EA sing the praises of their new ad platform, which is "transforming how brands connect with audiences". Companies looking to hock their wares can now pay EA to plaster themselves over any and all in-game advertising slots.

I can't be the only one who was looking at all the hours people were racking up in EA Sports FC, Madden NFL, and The Sims and thinking, "But if they're spending all their time here, when will they meaningfully engage with the brands?". I don't think I'll be alone in saying thank goodness someone is thinking of those poor brands. I was in the pub just the other day, and I saw Nike sidling up to people at the bar, opening his moth-eaten tick-emblazoned coat, and saying "C'mon, look at my flyer". Bless EA for giving the chap a helping hand.

Though, I am perhaps being a little disingenuous, after all, it's not only billboards. According to the press release, Madden NFL players play "the equivalent of 23,000 NFL seasons every day" and "this scale and frequency of engagement create meaningful opportunities for brands to connect with audiences through authentic, interactive experiences." One such example of these authentic experiences is Mountain Dew's Dew University. Which EA say comes with "a custom stadium, mascot, and reward ecosystem." I've not searched this out, but I am assuming play stops every minute for a slo-mo cam of the players enjoying a Dew Guzzle® and you can urge your players to even greater heights of play by Pumping Them Up® with a salvo from the Dew Cannon®.

I probably shouldn't be as down on this scheme as I am, after all, it's just EA proudly announcing what it's always done. Who else remembers The Sims 2's H&M and IKEA DLC packs, in which EA presumably took money from the companies before then selling it onto players to create "authentic, interactive experiences"? Though, considering they couldn't renew the IKEA license for The Sims 2 Legacy Collection, perhaps they did in fact pay IKEA for that slice of verisimilitude.

It just gets my hackles up that this is all presented as a means of making a game more authentic or that these ad blocks are of benefit to the games' players. As opposed to a likely necessary new injection of cash to fund the coming pile of debt if the leveraged buyout of the company goes ahead. The sale puts $20 billion of debt onto EA's books, and they've got to pay that down somehow.

We are seeing across the industry that growing piles of debt on companies' books aren't making them develop more exciting or creative games, but look for ways to remain in the middle ground and squeeze out more cash from their products. In a recent interview with the New York Times' Hard Fork Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said that Microsoft have "not been monetising" their games properly, complaining that "there’s more monetisation of Xbox games happening on YouTube than at Microsoft.” Claiming they have been "subsidising" entertainment. That thinking may lead to 'creative' advertising, but it's not leading to more creative games.

We know that Microsoft are planning a "reset" and at the end of this month they are likely to lay off many staff, close studios, and cancel more games that aren't in the profitable middle ground. Just yesterday, two heads of Xbox game studios announced their departure – maybe pushed, maybe jumping ship, either way a sign of more to come – and three of Microsoft's gaming studios are on the chopping block. Including the never mainstream Double Fine.

If EA's new advertising platform does bring in a glut of new money, is that going to be used to fund more creative games? Or, is it more likely to encourage a brand manager on Battlefield to suggest they create a level set in a stadium where, as EA put it in their press release, brands can "come to play alongside [players]"?



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/ea-the-big-brains-behind-the-sims-2-hm-fashion-stuff-announce-theyre-transforming-how-brands-connect-with-audiences-with-in-game-billboards

item.title: GTA 6 Release Date Confirmed By Take-Two Boss In The Strangest Way
item.content:encoded:
People are understandably skeptical about Grand Theft Auto VI‘s November 19, 2026 release date, not least because two previous launch dates have been abandoned. Rockstar revealed in May 2024 that it would no longer release as planned in fall 2025, but rather May 26, 2026. Then in November of the same year it was pushed back yet another six months to the current promised date. People are on tenterhooks waiting to find out if it will slip yet again, 13 years on since the release of the previous entry in the franchise. But an ambush interview of Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick by Tiktoker The School of Hard Knocks has once more affirmed there will be no more delays.

The TikTok channel appears to be a pretty gross outlet for interviewing billionaires to ask them how they got rich “to help the younger generation,” and ew. In this particular instance, what looks like it should be a staged ambush does appear to be pretty genuine, given the Tiktoker’s apparent surprise to learn who Zelnick is and the company he runs. Who knows, maybe he can act a bit, but his question of “When is GTA 6 coming out man?” (captioned “GTA XI,” but Roman numerals, man) really doesn’t come across as someone demanding to know if a promise is being kept but rather as someone genuinely not knowing.

“November 19,” Zelnick replies, before being bombarded with further goggle-eyed questions. “What was the most amount of money you made in a single year?” he asks the CEO, who then gives Take Two’s revenue figures for 2025 ($6.7 billion) and this year’s projected figure (“a little over $8 billion”). The TikToker then asked for a business lesson he wasn’t taught at Harvard, to which the owner of the company that releases NBA 2K games every year says, “Never compromise your integrity. It’s the only thing you have.”

Asked if people should “chase their passion,” Zelnick gives a slightly nuanced response. “I think that’s terrible advice, half the time. People should chase the intersection of what they’re good at and what they like to do, with emphasis on what you’re good at.” It’s still spoken from a position of privilege, but it’s not the worst advice. But then he goes straight back to Rich Guy Bullshit by adding, “Do that and the world will probably open up to you.” No dude, it opened up to you, a member of the self-selecting minuscule minority of those who got stupidly lucky.

But there we are. Even off the cuff, it seems Take-Two are convinced Rockstar are going to hit this deadline with just five months left to go. Could it actually be true? There would be so many developers and publishers so very pissed off if it weren’t!



https://kotaku.com/gta-6-grand-theft-auto-release-date-confirmed-take-two-rockstar-2000707059

item.title: Hellraiser: Revival Release Date Revealed Alongside Horrifying New Trailer
item.content:encoded:
Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival has been given an official release date by Saber Interactive and Boss Team Games. The announcement landed today alongside a visceral, horrifying, and downright gratuitous trailer that is reserved exclusively for the strong of heart and mind.

This promises to be one of the most violent games of the year, and the trailer is so graphic that I can’t even embed it below.

If you haven’t already, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android:

  • Android Link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.insidergaming.app
  • iOS Link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/insider-gaming/id6753984648

Hellraiser: Revival Coming In Time for Halloween

Hellraiser: Revival is headed to XBOX, PlayStation 5, and PC platforms on October 8, 2026. That means it’ll be one of Halloween’s must-buy horror games, and judging from what’s coming, those with a penchant for gory titles will fall in love with this game.

When Hellraiser: Revival launches, it’ll come with two editions: standard and deluxe. Pre-order bonuses are being lined up in the form of cosmetics, and those with a love for all this horror can invest a little deeper to secure the coveted collector’s edition.

In terms of pricing, the standard edition will carry an amicable price tag of just $39.99, while the deluxe edition will set you back $49.99.

The collector’s edition, which has three tiers, will run you up a heftier bill.

The lowest tier of the collector’s edition, the Genesis Edition, has a $129.99 price tag, but the top-shelf version will cost $249.99. This ultimate tier, the Signed Pinhead Edition, comes with a huge amount of additional content and items, and it’s limited to just 1,500 copies.

Here’s the release date trailer, but it’s the slightly censored, toned-down version and not the red band version, which includes full frontal nudity and extreme violence and gore:

Are you looking forward to Hellraiser: Revival, you sick puppy? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that ARC Raiders has (kind of) a new PvE map condition

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: “I just don’t give a f**k about anything” – w0xic on Aurora Roster Rumors
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming spoke to legendary Turkish AWPer Özgür ‘w0xic’ Eker ahead of the IEM Cologne 2026 Major Playoffs. w0xic speaks about Aurora’s Major run, the roster rumors swirling around the team, and his part in the iconic 2019 mousesports roster.

How does it feel to going to the Lanxess?

It’s good. I mean, the major playoffs are always good and giving you exciting vibes that if you can reach the quarters, you can reach the final too.

So I’m happy overall that we made the playoffs in the biggest tournament in the world.

Do you feel like you got an easy run to the playoffs?

Yeah, I could tell. I think we played only Spirit, who are a higher level than others. But I think 9z too was great, but I think we did a good job on that game.

I think one of the up-and-coming tier one teams to be scared of in the scene.

But yeah, overall, we didn’t face Vitality, we didn’t face NAVI, and the rest of the boys, like Mouz, so this is how it is.

Sometimes, sometimes it’s this way, and we were always playing very tough opponents every time, game after game, and it became something like this is because it depends on the other team’s results.

What do you think has changed at this event, because you look more relaxed?

We got used to it because we have been playing together for enough time, but soulfly just came as a new member. He’s been here for five, six months.

So I think we just got used to each other as well with the team members, and also the atmosphere of the events and everything. And also the rest of the people, just giving everything to reach one more step further, because there is nothing to lose.

You know, we have been reaching the quarter, reaching the final, but we were always losing it in the end.

Only one tournament that we were able to win and the rest, it was just kind of an achievement and at the same time a failure for us. So I think we just said we’ll do our best, and that’s it. If it goes nowhere, it doesn’t matter. We’ll do our best.

You guys have been working with a mental coach. What kind of lessons do you think you’ve taken from that?

Personally, I have been already working in my previous teams as well with a mental performance coach. And I think it’s always good to have someone positive around you, always. Also, understanding the human being and everything, as a mindset.

Everyone is taking something different from that person, I think. I’m happy to work with it because there is always one face that always you can share the problems and you can talk privately. And also you can get positive vibes always from that person.

But I don’t know for the others what they are taking because it’s like confidential things.

So it’s more individual?

More individual, yes. This is more of an individual thing.

In terms of the team, there have been rumors about Aurora changing players. How have you kind of dealt with that?

I just don’t give a fuck about anything. I’m just trying to do my job.

It doesn’t matter I’m here or somewhere else. I need to give my everything to show, and also satisfy myself, and show the people as well that I can do anything in any pressure. It doesn’t matter.

It’s like I’m in or out. It’s not an important thing. This is now; I’m here. I’m just mainly focusing on this one.

If I’m there in next, next one, I’ll focus on next one. So I’m going to talk a bit about you individually.

Obviously, you had that amazing clutch the other day against 9z. I feel like maybe this year you’ve been given a bit more freedom to be more expressive. Do you think that’s true?

Personally, I don’t think that there is a special thing. It’s just what you have inside, and it just comes out with the individual work and teamwork, and also finding your spot inside the team and position-wise and everything.

I think that can give you a very great performance, but also make you down because you are trying to do something for your team.

So, sometimes everything is going right, and you can have like successful frags, clutches, and everything. So in the 9z game, everything was kind of right for our own way, and also my way.

You’re facing BetBoom first. How would you assess their strengths?

They are really playing great lately. So, as people know, they qualified as well for the playoffs, they are a kind of tough opponent.

They did a great job with Boombl4 coming back, and I think they’re playing good as a team. Also, tactically. There are experienced players, well, not some of them that much. But I think somehow they are really good, like 9Z.

Okay. And in terms of, I just want to ask you about, you know, obviously, you know, you’ve had an amazing career, you know, and I’m sure that right now you’re looking forward to this event and stuff like that. But like, one of the things that kind of constantly comes up in my kind of social media feed is people talking about that 2019 mouse, you know, roster.

More info here: IEM Cologne 2026 Major Playoff Preview

Yeah. Like, is that something like, do you feel like, like in retrospect that that was a special roster, like a special time?

I think that was a special roster, and it was a special time for all the players, for ropz, for frozen, for me. We were so young and upcoming rising stars in the scene.

I think it’s all of them is combined well under the Karrigan wings. He was one of the best in-game leaders in the world.

So, I think that was a kind of special thing for me to be witnessed and also the rest of the guys as well. So that’s why it was, it was good times.

How do you think it helped you develop as a player?

It’s, yeah, you’re learning too much because you are attending so many tournaments. You’re playing really good players next to you, and you are just attending winning events.

Of course, you’re learning the pressure, learning how to play in front of people. Also learning how the team things are working.

You know, inside the car, there are like many things. If some of them are not there, the car is not working. So every piece is how it should be standing. That’s what you’re learning it basically.

End of interview

Let us know your IEM Cologne 2026 Major predictions on Insider Gaming’s Discord!


For other news, see the League of Legends MSI play-ins bracket.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

item.title: Fortnite Maker Confirms How It Uses Generative AI to Help Create Concept Art and Designs for New Skins
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite maker Epic Games has offered a rare glimpse behind the scenes at how it designs new character skins and in-game locations — and shown how certain stages involve the use of generative AI tools.

In a video published to the Unreal Engine YouTube channel, Epic Games shows how a new Fortnite character is designed and iterated on by hand before being modified using an AI prompt to look more like a 3D model.

The video makes a clear distinction between the ideation stage and later changes made by AI generation — and all of this still in the concept art stage, before any asset is recreated in-game. But the footage also shows how AI generation will create unwanted additions or errors, which then must be identified and corrected in a further design pass by the (human) artist.

"The design is king, AI can generate generic stuff all day, but that's not what we're doing here," an Epic Games staff member says. "It just skips ahead in the timeline so [the artist] can focus on honing in on the design and crafting it exactly how he wants it to be."

The video's publication follows repeated questions from Fortnite fans over potential AI use for certain in-game assets — such as a poster showing a nine-toed character in a hammock — that the company has previously kept quiet over answering.

While the video makes clear that AI isn't used for designing characters from the ground up, it also makes clear that generative AI use is now a part of the company's workflow — and opens up the possibility that mistakes could still get missed within subsequent human checks.

We Build LEGO Fortnite Battle Bus

When designing concept art for in-game locations, it's a similar process. Sketches are drawn by hand in Photoshop, then recreated in 3D via the commonly-used 3D modelling tool Blender. Images from here are then adapted within Photoshop using AI prompts to explore alternative takes, such as day or night versions of the same scene, or to add destruction from a meteor strike.

"At every stage of the design, artists continue to polish and refine, but now teams can revise faster, so artists have more oppurtunities to explore," Epic says.

"All along the way there are continual reviews, before anything makes it into our games, and artists are careful to respect originality, track providence of their work, and ensure the finished product meets Epic's high quality standards."

Epic Games is no stranger to AI technology, of course, having previously used generative speech technology to reproduce James Earl Jones' Darth Vader portrayal. But despite having the rights and approval of Disney, the character's inclusion proved controversial, especially as players quickly began making Vader say things more aligned with the dark side of the Force.

Last year, Epic Games boss Tim Sweeney suggested that Valve should ditch Steam's AI Generated Content Disclosure label for games, as he believes AI use will become so ubiquitous it will make any warning redundant. "Why stop at AI use?" Sweeney wrote on social media. "We could have mandatory disclosures for what shampoo brand the developer uses. Customers deserve to know lol.

"It doesn’t matter any more,” he continued. "The AI tag is relevant to art exhibits for authorship disclosure, and to digital content licensing marketplaces where buyers need to understand the rights situation. It makes no sense for game stores, where AI will be involved in nearly all future production.”

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social



https://www.ign.com/articles/fortnite-maker-confirms-how-it-uses-generative-ai-to-help-create-concept-art-and-designs-for-new-skins

item.title: Hellraiser: Revival hooks a release date with trailer full of Doom-like glory kills and otherworldly powers
item.content:encoded:
It's Pinheaded for Halloween.

Clive Barker's Hellraiser: Revival was announced a year ago, and developer diaries have fed us scraps of information since, but we've been lacking a release date until now. Hellraiser: Revival launches on PC (Steam), PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X/S on 8th October, 2026.

The date came from a gruesome new trailer on the game's official website and YouTube, which does a better job than other previews of showing off how the game actually plays.

From the ability to invoke the Cenobites' hooks - the iconic extra-dimensional beings from the movies and the original novella - to using pyrokinesis, the combat loop goes well beyond simply shooting at and smacking demonic horrors until they're, um, dead again. The Genesis Configuration box used by the protagonist - which is different from the Lament cube that invokes the Cenobites in the films - can also be used to help with puzzles, so there's at least a promise of more varied gameplay.

The game, which has been described as sexually explicit, violent, and gory, will be available in two different editions: Standard ($39.99) and Deluxe ($49.99). Regional pricing hasn't been revealed at the time of writing, but Hellraiser: Revival is targeting a lower price point than other high-profile releases, which could help its case in the run-up to a Halloween season that's looking increasingly busy as GTA 6's November launch looms.

There's also mention of two different Collector's Editions coming on the official website, but details on their contents aren't available yet.

Described as a first-person survival horror game, Hellraiser: Revival is an original story which follows Aidan, a regular guy who must use the Genesis Configuration after coming in contact with the Cenobites to help his girlfriend escape from the Labyrinth dimension. The game has been described as a messed-up "love story" in the past, though I'd be surprised if it has a happy ending.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/hellraiser-revival-release-date-trailer

item.title: After second season success, the Twisted Metal TV show is back with the legendary Mark Hammill in a familiar role
item.content:encoded:
Clowns to the left of me, jokers to the right.

Mark Hamill has joined the cast of Peacock and Sony Pictures Television's Twisted Metal adaptation. The actor will have a recurring role in the show's third season.

Hamill - known for his portrayal of Luke Skywalker in the Star Wars series, and his extensive voice acting work, including his acclaimed take on Batman's Joker in numerous animations and games - will play Pope Charlie Kane in season three of Twisted Metal. Please be aware of potential show spoilers below.

Those who have played the games will know Charlie Kane is the father of Sweet Tooth, Twisted Metal's clown-themed killer with a flaming head. While Sweet Tooth, aka Needles Kane, drives an ice cream truck, Charlie Kane is the driver of Yellow Jacket, which is often depicted as a taxi-like vehicle.

As reported by Deadline, the TV series' version of Charlie Kane will be the leader (or pope) of a faction known as The Eastern Sovereignty. "For Mark Hamill to come into this world with us is amazing," said Anthony Mackie, who plays the show's protagonist John Doe and serves as an executive producer. "He's someone I've admired for a long time - a true legend who's influenced generations of actors. Being able to now call him a castmate is an incredible honour, and we're excited to see what he brings to the world of Twisted Metal."

In addition to Mackie, Twisted Metal's main cast includes Joe Seanoa as Sweet Tooth (although the character is voiced by Will Arnett), Stephanie Beatriz as Quiet, and Anthony Carrigan as Calypso. The show was renewed for a third season in November, after its second season registered 993m minutes viewed on Peacock. At the time of its renewal, Peacock said the show had become its "second highest-reaching original returning scripted season".

The first season of Twisted Metal was nominated for Best Adaptation at the 2023 Game Awards, but the accolade ended up going to HBO's The Last of Us TV series.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/twisted-metal-season-three-mark-hamill

item.title: Moon Games CEO Says XBOX Leadership Was ‘Too Focused on Past’
item.content:encoded:
Moon Games Studio CEO Thomas Mahler has stated that XBOX leadership was “too focused on the past,” responding on social media to several reported closures by XBOX Game Studios.

Ninja Theory, the studio behind Hellblade, is being closed just days after revealing a new title at the XBOX Games Showcase, while Double Fine and other studios are seeking deals with XBOX to avoid closure.

The closures come amid sweeping changes from new XBOX CEO Asha Sharma, but Mahler believes that all the turmoil will ultimately be positive for the company.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android. 

  • Android Link
  • iOS Link

Mahler Says XBOX Leadership ‘Too Focused on The Past’

In a lengthy post on X, Mahler explained how the ‘heartbreaking’ changes could be good for games and gamers, as XBOX “has struggled for a long time to identify, empower, and protect the key creative people and teams who could have kept the brand at the top.”

He claims that during the development of Ori and the Blind Forest, and its sequel, Will of the Wisps, it was “clear that the main focus was still Halo, Gears [of War], and Forza.”

  • More Insider Gaming News: Embark Makes Long-Awaited Free Loadout Change to ARC Raiders

Mahler had hoped that Microsoft would “turn Ori into their Mickey Mouse or Mario-esque mascot,” claiming he “emailed Phil [Spencer] plenty of times that XBOX should have their eyes on delivering magical experiences for kids and families as well, like Disney and Nintendo always have.”

However, he says, “the powers to be were probably too focused on the past for that to happen.”

XBOX still has a big year ahead, with the releases of Halo: Campaign Evolved and Gears of War: E-Day on the horizon, fresh off the back of Forza Horizon 6, and further changes are expected in the months ahead.

Do you agree with Thomas Mahler’s views on XBOX’s approach? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at Double Fine addressing the XBOX shutdown situation.

item.title: UK social media ban will restrict 'high-risk' features on game platforms
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 16, 2026

3 Min Read

Photo by Taylor R on Unsplash

The UK government has confirmed it will ban social media for under 16s and impose some restrictions on video game platforms that offer communications and livestreaming functionality.

The move comes after the government ran a national consultation from March to May 2026 that it said showed "overwhelming public demand for action" with 9 out of 10 parents backing a social media ban for children aged 16 and under.

Although the government noted that children in that age group will still be able to "access the online world safely for learning, news, games, and staying in touch with known friends"—meaning video games have avoided an outright ban—it explained "high-risk features" including livestreaming and communications with strangers will be restricted on video game platforms.

"This will be backed up by stronger requirements for age checks on platforms," adds an explainer on the UK government website.

Related:Report: Multiple Xbox game studio heads negotiating with Xbox to avoid closure

It's currently unclear how the UK government will seek to regulate those platforms—or which video game platforms will be impacted. 

"Yes it's hard. Hard to legislate for. Hard to regulate. Hard to enforce," said UK prime minister Keir Starmer in a Sky News broadcast. "That's why we sought a wide range of views on this. That's why we listened to people. Had a conversation. We looked carefully at the evidence. Learned from countries like Australia, that are taking similar steps.

"We're not just bringing forward a ban. We're going further. We're taking world-leading action on gaming services and live streaming platforms, where at the moment strangers can contact any child unchecked. Just think about that. Is there a situation in the offline world where you would just let your child pair up with a stranger? An adult that you don't know anything about. No. So we're taking action on that."

UK video game industry trade body Ukie responded to the news by welcoming the decision to distinguish video games from social media in the proposed restrictions for under 16s, and said it has offered to act as a technical partner with the government to co-create the regulations that will apply to game platforms.

The organization also stressed that many video game platforms already include parental controls that can be used to restrict young users from chatting with strangers, and noted that new age rating criteria being introduced by PEGI will result in titles with unrestricted communication features being automatically rated PEGI 18 regardless of their content.

As noted by the BBC earlier this year, Australian internet regulator eSafety has suggested the country's own social media ban has fallen short of expectations due to a lack of compliance from those companies impacted.

Related:Report: Two former Rare execs are leaving Xbox

In another report from the BBC published in May, Scotland's children commissioner Nicola Killean questioned the effectiveness of a ban and said it could even drive children towards more dangerous corners of the internet. 

The UK government has pledged to outline how the ban will be implemented in the coming months and said the legislation will come into effect from Spring 2027. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/uk-social-media-ban-will-restrict-high-risk-features-on-game-platforms

item.title: CD Projekt reportedly has a second multiplayer Witcher game in development that's free-to-play and coming to mobile and PC
item.content:encoded:
As The Witcher 4's development continues and we await a release date for The Witcher 3's next expansion - its first DLC in ten years - a new report has claimed developer CD Projekt has a second, previously unannounced multiplayer Witcher game in the works.

That's according to MP1st, whose sources claim this second multiplayer project - described as a free-to-play co-op action-RPG - is a separate game to CD Projekt's long-in-the-works Project Sirius.

Supposedly, the free-to-play title is set in the year 1230, "during a time when Geralt was still a young witcher", and borrows elements from the main series' combat and progression systems. "Gameplay-wise, the title will take you on contracts to kill monsters across several environments," MP1st wrote. "A skill-based system drives the combat mechanics, letting you choose abilities from different witcher schools, shaping your moveset by mastering Signs and brewing potions." It's also claimed players can create and customise their own witchers.

MP1st's sources also claim the free-to-play game is specifically targeting PC and mobile devices - unlike Project Sirius, which is described as a larger title coming to PC and consoles.

There's no word of a developer, but it's perhaps notable that CD Projekt entered into a deal with Saudi-backed mobile developer Scopely last year to "develop a game set within one of [the studio's] IPs". That followed confirmation in 2024 it was considering licensing opportunities to bring The Witcher and Cyberpunk to mobile devices.

We reached out to CD Projekt Red for comment on the report.

Mentioned in this article

item.title: Rejoice! The Original Switch eShop Isn't Awful Anymore
item.content:encoded:
Well, except for the 'eSlop'

Nintendo just dropped system updates for the Switch and Switch 2, and while the latter is fairly inconsequential for a lot of folks, the former does something pretty spectacular: it fixes the eShop.

Yes, the original Switch eShop has been redesigned to bring it more in line with the Switch 2, ditching the browser-based setup for a dedicated app. As noted by Daniel Vuckovic from Vooks, the eShop now runs at a much improved speed.

You'll also note in the below footage that the overall design is much darker. This is optional, and is applied automatically if you have the 'Basic Dark' theme chosen for the Switch homepage.

It's much better, right? The speed at which you are able to scroll through the Current Offers tab is frankly ridiculous compared to how slow it used to be.

This is a very welcome improvement, then, and one that's been a long time coming. All we need now is for Nintendo to purge some of the more egregious 'eSlop' titles. The eShop still plays host to dozens upon dozens of cheap games that rely heavily on AI-generated key art, descriptions, and sometimes, full game assets.

Many go so far as to blatantly copy more well-known, popular titles as a way to have more visibility on the eShop and dupe customers into purchases.

What do you make of the improved eShop on Switch? Let us know with a comment down below.

[source bsky.app]

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile

item.title: Assassin's Creed Shadows' final update adds one last story mission and a wearable Black Flag Resynced advert
item.content:encoded:
Mostly decent open-world ninja sim Assassin’s Creed Shadows is getting its last big update today, as Ubisoft begin the process of shooing players away from the year-old RPG and towards the gleaming, nipple-adding newness of Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag Resynced. The Shadows patch even includes an unlockable outfit based on Edward Kenway’s pirate garb, displaced only 150 or so years out of its appropriate time period for your synergistic brand awareness pleasure.

The update’s meatier contents come in the form of a concluding story mission, Black Tides, in which a pair of high-ranking Templar types arrive in Japan for one final attempt on the lives of dual protagonists Naoe and Yasuke. There’s also a new endgame challenge mode, Domains, which’ll drop you in extra-tricky Animus arenas built to "push you out of your comfort zone and rethink your loadouts." There’s some unique gear in it for those who undergo sufficient loadout-rethinking, including a hatchet that looks suspiciously straight out of Assassin’s Creed III. Maybe they’ll remake that one next.

The Black Flag Resynced crossover is at least a tit-for-tat deal: progressing through the rewards tree in Shadows will also unlock some Japan-themed gear you can eventually use in Black Flag Resynced, should you pick it up next month, including some equally silly ninja and samurai outfits to skulk around the Caribbean in. Unfortunately, associate game director Simon Lemay-Comtois pitches this system in the above video with the phrase "You get to work towards those rewards," a series of words that should probably never exist in that specific order.

The update’s out today, June 16th, at 3pm BST / 10am EST / 7am PST. Black Flag Resynced is releasing on July 9th, hopefully after fixing the wonkiness that Julian found in his (not entirely areola-centred) preview. While neither Shadows’ nor Black Flag Resync’s lead studios, respectively Ubisoft Quebec and Ubisoft Singapore, are seemingly affected, this update also comes days after Ubisoft reportedly chose to close down their Winnipeg and Belgrade studios while significantly restructuring Ubisoft Barcelona. That in turn followed just months after another major round of layoffs and game cancellations that prompted strike action from unionised Ubi staff.

item.title: Embark Makes Long-Awaited Free Loadout Change to ARC Raiders
item.content:encoded:
If you’re any kind of ARC Raiders fan, you’ll know the situation with free loadouts, a mechanic in the game that allows users to enter a raid with a randomized kit. It’s a point of contention, as it has almost no downsides for the user taking the kit, but plenty of concerns for those who meet said player.

For the longest time, players more geared towards taking in ‘paid kits’ and running into other players to fight them have requested Embark Studios examine how they manage this mechanic, and now, months after launch, that has finally happened.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android. 

  • Android Link: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.insidergaming.app
  • iOS Link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/insider-gaming/id6753984648

Free Loadouts Being Removed (Kind of) From ARC Raiders

In the latest ARC Raiders update, Embark Studios revealed it’s testing the removal of free loadouts, but only under certain conditions.

Those conditions are map conditions, and for the next three weeks, anyone loading into the Night Raid or Close Scrutiny conditions will find that they can only do so if they’re running a paid kit. There will be no free kit functionality on these map conditions for the next three weeks.

This is a test-bed event, with Embark Studios assessing how players react to this action before presumably rolling it out across other map conditions and criteria.

In an ideal world (my world, I guess), you’d have no free loadouts on any map condition, leaving only the players with paid kits and a bit of effort in their build to saunter into these more challenging environments.

  • RELATED: ARC Raiders PvE Map Condition Revealed

The team wrote in the patch notes for the 1.33.0 update for ARC Raiders:

We are disabling free loadouts for Night Raid and Close Scrutiny to increase the barrier of entry so that players commit to appropriate risk and reward in these scenarios that have better loot.

This is a time limited change that we will be testing for the next three weeks. We may add this limitation to more map conditions in the future after we’ve observed its impact and analyzed your feedback.

Do you think this change is a huge win? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Assassin’s Creed Shadows’ final update has been revealed and sign up to the Insider Gaming newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: When Does Pokémon Champions Mobile Release? Exact Start Time & Date
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Champions is entering a new era with the long-awaited mobile launch of the title, bringing a flood of new players into the game, and a major update coincides with the drop.

Released earlier this year on Nintendo Switch devices, Pokémon Champions has become the format for Pokémon’s competitive VGC scene, and the doors are now set to open for those on mobile.

Before going any further, download the free Insider Gaming App for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android. 

  • Android Link
  • iOS Link

When Does Pokémon Champions Release on Mobile?

The release of Pokémon Champions on mobile is set for Wednesday, June 17, but time zone differences mean the exact date does vary.

For those in North and South America, Pokémon Champions releases on mobile in the late evening on June 16, while those in Europe will see the drop in the early hours of the morning on June 17.

  • More Pokémon: Pokémon Moving North American International Championship to Chicago From 2027

In the eastern part of the world, Pokémon Champions’ mobile release will vary from market to market, and the drop comes alongside a new update to the title on all devices.

Alongside the Pokémon Champions mobile release, the game shifts to Regulation M-B, the first change in ruleset since the title first released, coming fresh off the back of the North American International Championship at the weekend.

What Time Does Pokémon Champions Mobile Release?

Pokémon Champions’ mobile debut has a simultaneous release worldwide, becoming available across the globe at exactly the same moment.

This is instead of a staggered release, like many game releases, which sees titles drop in the likes of New Zealand and Australia first. However, such an approach doesn’t work for a major live title like Pokémon Champions.

  • More Pokémon: Pokémon Champions Mobile and Mega Raichu Release Confirmed

The exact timing varies significantly, with some set for a late night if they want to jump into Pokémon Champions on mobile, or experience the new update immediately, while others see the drop happen around lunchtime.

Take a look at the exact Pokémon Champions Mobile release date across all major time zones below.

Are you excited by the release of Pokémon Champions on mobile? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.
